userData.mode: sf2 reduced attack baked from source-time remap

sourceReduction.goal: Street Fighter 2 style discrete read poses with procedural 3D easing between anchors.
sourceReduction.timingPolicy: preserve source keyframe burst around snap, then hold contact before recovery

spriteFrames.description: start extended leg, low foot, readable shift
guard loaded leg, low foot, snappy transition
guard loaded leg, low foot, snappy transition
anticipation full leg extension, high foot, snappy transition
strike extended leg, high foot, snappy transition
strike extended leg, high foot, snappy transition
strike extended leg, high foot, snappy transition
strike full leg extension, mid foot, snappy transition
strike full leg extension, mid foot, snappy transition
strike extended leg, low foot, held pose
recovery extended leg, low foot, readable shift
start extended leg, low foot, readable shift

contactExaggeration.reason: Make the AxeKick contact frame read as a crushing downward impact: hips drop, body drives through, torso compresses, and recovery rises out of a committed overextension.

headDiscipline.reason: Do not procedurally counter-rotate Ares spine/head for AxeKick; prior head-discipline bakes visibly destroyed torso appeal and made the pose hunch away from center.
headDiscipline.rule: Preserve the source torso/head relationship. Critique head discipline from visual evidence, but do not bake synthetic spine/head offsets unless a future visual test proves the full pose improves.

headGazeStabilization.reason: Prevent AxeKick contact from reading as a fighter looking down at the floor. Preserve hips/spine/source torso pose and correct only local Neck/Head gaze.

contactGuardStabilization.reason: Counter the lifted-head/low-fist read at AxeKick contact by keeping fists near torso guard height. Preserve hips, spine, legs, neck, and head.

rules.label: striking foot cannot pop upward during late recovery
striking foot vertical delta cannot pop between recovery frames
striking foot forward motion cannot jump between recovery frames

windows.reason: Stationary AxeKick should keep the support foot rock steady in world space while the body and striking leg move around that anchor.

smashRealisticSteering.reason: Use Ganondorf as the realistic-body-cheat benchmark for heavy kicks: big silhouette change, hips-first commitment, and chest/shoulder compression before release without turning the body into rubber.
do: lead power from hips through torso into the limb
hold a screenshot-worthy contact or apex read when the move identity needs it
favor silhouette expansion over obvious bone scaling

squashStretchLayer.reason: Use a restrained Smash-style squash/stretch settle on AxeKick so the held contact compresses into the recovery instead of reading as a hard pose cut to idle.

sourceOverlays.reason: AxeKick keeps its source leg/root pose while Headbutt contributes phase-shaped upper-limb energy: guarded chamber, counterbalance through apex, braced pull at contact.
